title: Turning Raw Market Data into Predictive Models Using an Advanced Ingestion Pipeline
subtitle: MQL5→Flask Data Pipeline, Feature Engineering (MACD/RSI/ATR/Kalman/Prophet), Gradient Boosting
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: MEDIUM EXTRACT — spike detection feature engineering, Kalman slope, Prophet delta, chunked data upload pattern, label threshold THRESH=0.15%. We already have Python-native access — skip the bridge. Feature set is reusable.

feature_engineering.kalman_slope: Kalman filter trend slope — noise-reduced price direction
feature_engineering.prophet_delta: Facebook Prophet 1h forecast - current price = expected move

labeling.logic: label=1 if close[+10] > close * 1.0015; label=-1 if close[+10] < close * 0.9985; else label=0

keep: Spike feature: range > 2× rolling(50) mean = simple, effective spike flag
Label threshold: 0.15% = 15pip move = our minimum meaningful signal
GBC config: 400 estimators, lr=0.05, depth=3 — validated hyperparameters
Kalman slope as noise-reduced trend direction (alternative to EMA3)
Chunked upload pattern with 14MB payload limit
discard: Flask bridge — we have direct Python access
improve: Add CRT candle type as feature: 'LR'/'SR'/'IB'/'OB' encoded as integer
Add zone_quality as feature: zone strength score 0-100
